title: felix_v1: int6+zstd, fp16 embed, MLP 3x, seq4096, sliding window eval
author: felix-ab
body:
## Summary

Stacking complementary techniques to maximize parameter efficiency within the 16MB artifact limit.

## Changes from baseline

### int6 quantization + zstd-22
- `QUANT_MAX=31` (6-bit range) stored in int8 containers — same memory layout, ~25% smaller values → better compression
- zlib level-9 replaced with zstd level-22 (~10-15% additional savings)
- Net effect: body weights compress to ~14.1MB instead of ~15.8MB for baseline

### fp16 embedding passthrough
- `tok_emb.weight` skips quantization and is stored as fp16 directly
- Embeddings are the most quantization-sensitive tensor (directly maps to logit quality)
- Cost: ~0.5MB at dim=512; worth it for quality insurance

### MLP 3x (default)
- `MLP_MULT=3` instead of 2
- Space freed by int6+zstd enables wider hidden layers within budget
- Adds ~4M parameters vs baseline at same artifact size

### seq4096 training (default)
- `TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096` instead of 1024
- Longer context enables model to learn longer-range dependencies
- Required for sliding window eval to show meaningful gains

### Sliding window eval
- `eval_val_sliding_window()` added: scores tokens with maximum left context
- Stride=512: each token scored with up to `seq_len` context tokens
- `forward_logits()` added to GPT class for logit access without loss reduction
- Runs after quantized roundtrip eval and logs bpb improvement delta
- Free gain at eval time — no artifact cost

## Artifact budget
```
Body: 21.4M params @ int6+zstd → ~14.1MB
Embedding: 512×1024 fp16 → 0.5MB  
Total: ~14.6MB (under 16MB)
```

## Local validation
- Baseline reproduces on Apple Silicon (M4 Pro)
- int6 quant gap confirmed <0.01 bpb on smoke test
- Script is syntax-clean, 1263 lines (under 1500 limit)
- `zstandard` falls back to `zlib` gracefully if not installed

## Status
Draft — awaiting H100 training run to report final val_bpb.
submission_readme:
# felix_v1

## Changes from baseline

### 1. int6 quantization (body weights)
- Default int8 [-127, 127] replaced with int6 [-31, 31]
- Same per-row scheme, same storage (int8 container), but 6-bit value range
- zlib → zstd level-22 compression (~10-15% smaller artifact)
- Result: ~25% smaller artifact for body weights

### 2. fp16 embedding passthrough
- Tied embedding / lm_head kept at fp16 instead of quantized
- Embeddings are the most quantization-sensitive tensor (directly affects logit quality)
- Net artifact cost vs int6 embedding: ~400KB at dim=512, worth the quality gain

### 3. MLP 3x (default)
- MLP_MULT=3 instead of 2
- Space freed by int6+zstd enables wider hidden layers within 16MB budget
- Model body: ~21.4M params at dim=512, 9 layers, mlp_mult=3 → fits in ~14MB int6+zstd

### 4. seq4096 training (default)
- TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096 instead of 1024
- Longer context during training → model learns longer-range dependencies
- Enables sliding window eval to show real gains

### 5. Sliding window eval
- After quantized roundtrip eval, runs sliding window evaluation (stride=512)
- Each token scored with seq_len tokens of left context instead of variable (0 to seq_len)
- Free bpb improvement — no artifact cost, uses eval compute budget
- Expected gain: ~0.034 bpb on well-trained models (per PR #88)

## Expected artifact size
- Body (21.4M params, int6): ~16MB × 0.75 = 16.0MB raw → ~14.1MB zstd-22
- Embedding (512×512, fp16): 0.5MB
- Total: ~14.6MB → safely under 16MB

## Config
All hyperparameters are env-var overridable. Defaults:
- MLP_MULT=3
- TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096
- WEIGHT_QUANT_BITS=6
- EMBED_QUANT_BITS=0 (fp16 passthrough)
- SLIDING_WINDOW_STRIDE=512
